title: Trema imbricatum
taxonomy: Trema imbricatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Salicaceae, commonly known as the willow family. It is a member of the genus Trema, which consists of several species of tropical and subtropical plants. The Trema genus is further classified under the order Malpighiales, a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The family Salicaceae is part of the clade Rosidae, which includes many other families of flowering plants. This classification system is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics.

intro: Trema imbricatum, also known as the East Indian copperleaf or the mangrove copperleaf, is a tropical tree species native to the coastal regions of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, thanks to its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
